message:
perf(fj-dispatch): vectorize vmap scan scalar folds

frankenjax-fnzr

files.patch:
@@ -91,7 +91,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenjax-fad","title":"Fix fuzzer input-count placeholders for control flow and multi-input primitives","description":"WHAT: Fix the hardcoded arity=1 placeholder for 10 primitives in crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/fuzz_targets/common.rs:257-268. The comment "Default placeholder" confirms these are wrong.
WHERE: The function primitive_input_count() at approximately line 200 of common.rs.
HOW: Set correct input counts based on each primitive definition:
  - DynamicUpdateSlice: 3 (operand, update, start_indices)
  - OneHot: 1 (indices tensor; num_classes is a param)
  - Cumsum: 1 (input tensor; axis is a param)
  - Cumprod: 1 (input tensor; axis is a param)
  - Sort: 1 (input tensor; dimension is a param)
  - Argsort: 1 (input tensor; dimension is a param)
  - Conv: 2 (lhs, rhs; strides/padding are params)
  - Cond: 3+ (predicate, true_operands, false_operands)
  - Scan: 2+ (init_carry, xs)
  - While: 1+ (init_state)
Note: Cond/Scan/While also need sub-jaxpr generation in the fuzzer, which is a harder problem. For this task, at minimum fix the numeric arity. Sub-jaxpr fuzzing can be a follow-up.
WHY: With arity=1, the fuzzer generates malformed inputs for these primitives, meaning they always fail at arity validation and never exercise the actual primitive logic. This is wasted fuzzing cycles.
TESTING: Run the fuzzer briefly to confirm it no longer panics on arity mismatches for these primitives.
ACCEPTANCE: The "Default placeholder" comment is removed. Each primitive has a documented, correct input count.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:27:23.366975095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:11:47.884300639Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:11:47.884125297Z","close_reason":"Implemented real primitive arity counts for parameterized conformance fuzz targets; added regression test; verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --all-targets","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenjax-fft","title":"Profile branched CPU scheduler fixture DAGs","description":"Follow-up from frankenjax-752. Current scheduler benchmarks cover chain/stress graphs and pairwise wide reductions; add fixture-derived branched DAG coverage to validate the 128-equation cutover and catch regressions on mixed fanout/fanin shapes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T05:15:15.808980533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T05:31:49.927340529Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T05:31:49.925444239Z","close_reason":"Added branched fan-in scheduler benchmarks, tensor-aware scalar wave gating, parity ledger evidence, and full validation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenjax-fij","title":"Implement complex pow in apply_complex_binary (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)","description":"WHAT: Add complex exponentiation to apply_complex_binary() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:145-164.
WHERE: The match arm at line 160 currently returns "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" for all binary ops except add/sub/mul/div.
HOW: Complex pow uses the identity (a+bi)^(c+di) = exp((c+di)*ln(a+bi)). Decompose: r=|z|, theta=atan2(b,a), ln(z)=(ln(r), theta), then exp of the product. For integer exponents, use repeated squaring for efficiency.
WHY: JAX supports complex pow natively. Without it, grad(f) fails for any traced program that involves complex exponentiation.
TESTING: Add tests in fj-lax for complex pow at several points: (1+i)^2 = 2i, (1+i)^(0.5), purely real complex pow matches real pow. Add numerical AD verification for complex pow VJP.
ACCEPTANCE: The "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" error no longer triggers for Primitive::Pow with complex inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:27:22.987041461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:59.161055668Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:59.160808239Z","close_reason":"Completed: implemented complex pow in fj-lax with integer fast path and direct evaluator tests; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -57,6 +57,16 @@ Audit timestamp: **2026-04-01** (`cargo fmt --all` passed locally; `cargo check
 - Re-baseline on the same host and target dir: `19.835us` (-44.011%).
 - Isomorphism proof: ordering preserved because switch branches are pure Jaxprs and selection still follows the original batch order; tie-breaking/clamping unchanged via the existing `scalar_to_switch_index` rules for negative and high indices; floating-point/RNG N/A for the measured integer branches; golden behavior guarded by primitive and sub-Jaxpr batched switch tests plus conformance `vmap(switch)` coverage.
 
+### 2026-04-28: `frankenjax-fnzr` primitive scalar-sequence `vmap(scan)` folds
+
+- Scope: `fj-dispatch` BatchTrace handling for primitive-form `Scan` when `vmap` maps scalar sequence bodies (`add`, `sub`, `mul`) over batched scalar carries and rank-1/rank-2 `xs`.
+- Baseline: Criterion `vmap_scan/shared_init_batched_xs_128x64` measured `313.44us` before the change.
+- Profile: the generic transpose-and-vectorized-scan attempt regressed to `462.99us`; the actual source hotspot was the generic per-batch fallback allocating/slicing 128 independent scan executions for a scalar row-fold workload.
+- Opportunity score: impact 4, confidence 5, effort 2 => 10.0.
+- Lever: fold scalar rows directly from the batched `xs` tensor for supported primitive scan bodies, preserving the existing fallback for higher-rank carries, unsupported body ops, and generalized control-flow scans.
+- Re-baseline on the same worker and target dir after narrowing the literal fast path to exact I64/F64 semantics: `35.901us` (-88.546%).
+- Isomorphism proof: ordering preserved by iterating scan positions in the same forward/reverse order per batch row; tie-breaking unchanged/N/A; floating-point follows the same left-fold operation order within each row; RNG N/A; behavior guarded by leading-axis, nonzero-axis, scalar-`xs`, and existing dispatch-suite `vmap_scan_batched_carry_and_xs` tests.
+
 ## Required Evidence Per Family
 
 1. Differential conformance report.

@@ -953,7 +953,7 @@ Both primitives have VJP and JVP rules. Gather's VJP produces a scatter (adjoint
 
 - **CPU-only backend.** GPU/TPU backends are not yet implemented. The CPU backend uses rayon for wave-parallel execution.
 - **No XLA lowering.** FrankenJAX evaluates through its own interpreter, not through XLA. This means we match JAX's mathematical semantics but not its compilation/optimization pipeline.
-- **Partial `vmap` + iterative control flow composition.** `vmap(scan(...))`, `vmap(while_loop(...))`, and similar iterative compositions need further work. Batched-index `vmap(switch(...))` uses branch-once batched evaluation with row-wise selection.
+- **Partial `vmap` + iterative control flow composition.** General functional `vmap(scan(...))`, `vmap(while_loop(...))`, and similar iterative compositions need further work. Primitive scalar-sequence `vmap(scan(...))` uses direct batched row folds, and batched-index `vmap(switch(...))` uses branch-once batched evaluation with row-wise selection.
